purpose: Write a single answer/property on a KC form. One field per call.

body_template.propertyKey: string — the renderProperty's key in its ORIGINAL CASE as seen in the GET
body_template.value: string — display text (for dropdowns, must match a floatieItemList entry)
body_template.valueKey: string — dropdown valueKey (empty string for free text)

concurrency_rule: SEQUENTIAL ONLY — parallel POSTs to the same form drop writes (server race condition).

multi_value_fields.description: Some properties (Risks on .{AREA}.ProgramSteps; PlannedAuditApproach single-option writes) accept multi-value via semicolon-joined values.
multi_value_fields.Risks_property: FULL-STATE REPLACEMENT. value/valueKey are semicolon-joined with TRAILING SEMI (e.g. 'A;B;C;'). To add one, send existing + new. To remove one, send existing minus that one.
multi_value_fields.PlannedAuditApproach: Single canonical valueKey per call (COMBINED, ANALYTICAL, INDEPTH). One POST per checkbox.
multi_value_fields.unknown_codes: Sending unknown codes in a semicolon-joined value returns 500 'Index was outside the bounds of the array'.

verification: Response echoes the full updated form — no separate verify GET needed for happy path. But after batch + submit, do one re-GET to confirm state==3.
script_function: scripts.kc.update_property(eng_guid, wp_id, payload, headers)  # caller is responsible for sequential dispatch
